title: Spectradyne nCS1: Sample measurement and device maintenence protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8m4vs","text":"This is a generic protocol for using the Spectradyne nCS1 resestive pulse sensing instrument to measure the diameter distribution and concentration of nanoparticles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"17f48","text":"The \"end run\" button must always be pressed before lowering the cartridge stage after a cartridge has been primed and/or a sample run on the cartridge. If the user is uncertain about whether the \"end run\" button has been previously selected, it can be selected a second time. The final step of the \"run cleaning cartridge\" procedure involves the \"end run\" process so it does not need to pressed after successful completion of the cleaning procedure.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":152}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"egh6c","text":"Particles must be in solution with dilute surfactant before being analyzed on nCS1","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"kp2a","text":"The \"run cleaning cartridge\" procedure doesn't need to be performed daily but is indicated after a long period where the instrument has not been run or any other reason where the user desires the fluidics of the instrument to be flushed.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"67pku","text":"The running buffer must be changed every two weeks. It is recommended that the user empties out the old buffer, rinses with filtered/pure water and allows to dry overnight before refilling.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":52}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7hrpi","text":"Generally, used cartridges can be discarded, but cleaning cartridges must be kept and reused. They should be visibly marked but if they are mixed with sample cartridges can be distinguished by the intricate maze-like channels etched into the glass on their bottom side","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":49,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8at77","text":"The full details of the preparation of sample buffer is left to the user as it has not been optimized but some potential options are to freshly filter sample buffer every other day, rinse plastics with filtered buffer before filling them, be aware of any dust or lint on surfaces used to prepare buffer, attempt to prepare buffer as sterily as possible (sterile plastics, using laminar flow hood)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ekjsi","text":"Whether or not 1% Tween in PBS affects the morphology of EVs is an area of current research and isn't yet known. To minimize surfactant effects on sample particles composed of lipids it is recommended that the time that samples are suspended in surfactant-containing buffer is minimized (prepare samples one at a time immediately before running)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b5c77","text":"It is easier to access the sample description stored in the nCS1 cartridge logsheet than the sample description typed into the nCS1 software","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bpnps","text":"For the purposes of record-keeping, each of the 8 cartridges in one mold can be given a number to distinguish among them","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cq1r2","text":"Once every month, empty water bottles and waste bottles, flush with filtered/pure water, and allow to dry overnight. Attempt to empty fluid lines attached to bottle caps","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cvvt","text":"It is possible the locking o-ring male/female fluidic connections may become damaged or worn over time and if the user is unable to connect them properly they should contact Spectradyne as having pressure leaks can affect the data","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dlsd8","text":"A single cartridge may be used in a dilution experiment, working from least to most concentrated sample, aspirating sample with a pipette after it is run. Carry-over effects are undetermined and may need to be investigated. For non-dilution series, a new cartridge is needed per sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ecj99","text":"Ensure the USB cable is firmly plugged into both the device and PC before turning on the instrument as any slight disconnection will interput a sample run ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ddjoc","text":"Check that the current software version is: 2.5.0.254","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fri8o","text":"Check the date listed on most recent calibration file: 5/13/2020","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":64}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: If running buffer is more than two weeks old, it must be prepared fresh.
Discard the old running buffer contained in the nCS1 buffer bottles.
Rinse out the buffer bottles with a small amount of purified/filtered water.
If there is no previously prepared 1% Tween-20 solution, prepare a 500 mL bottle of 1% Tween-20 by pipetting 5 mL of pure Tween-20 into a new sterile 500 mL bottle of DPBS.
Gently invert the bottle.
Let the bottle sit for at least 15 minutes while occasionally inverting until Tween-20 is entirely dissolved.
Filter half of the 1 % (v/v) Tween-20 solution with a 0.2 µm PES bottle-top filter.
Equally divide the filtered 1 % (v/v) Tween-20 solution into the two nCS1 buffer bottles.
Store running buffer at 4 °C and mark the date it was prepared so it can be changed in two weeks.
Turn on and log in to attached PC.
Obtain nCS1 buffer bottles and water bottles from the 4 °C fridge.

Attach the nCS1 buffer bottles to their indicated fluid lines on the side of the instrument. For all bottles, when refilling, be sure to screw the caps on tightly. Loose caps on the buffer bottles may cause pressure leaks that will result in the system from pressurizing and/or preventing chips from priming.
Turn on the nCS1 with the power switch on the back of the instrument.
Open the nCS1 software.

The bar at the top of the software should be green with the word "Ready" displayed. If not, shut off the nCS1, restart the PC and then repeat the power up process, ensuring the software is only opened after the nCS1 is powered on and properly connected via its USB cable.
If the user determines the device needs to be cleaned, obtain a cleaning cartridge. We recommend to run the cleaning cartridge at least once when turning on the instrument.
Properly position cartridge in slot on stage. The blue indicator light and the blue ring light should both illuminate.
Press the outside button to raise the stage before clicking 'run cleaning cartridge' in the software.
Wait about three minutes for routine to finish.
Lower stage by pressing metal button and return cartridge to safe storage place.

Prepare a 1.0 % (v/v) solution of Tween-20 in Thermo Fisher Gibco Sterile DPBS (identical to the running buffer) or a 0.1 % (v/v) solution of BSA in Thermo Fisher Gibco Sterile DPBS, ensuring ample time given to allow solids to dissolve. PBST (PBS with 1% Tween-20, filtered) should be used unless investigating buffer effects on lipid particles, as it is the default recommended buffer. Researchers with Edwin van der Pol's group have investigated other methods of removing small particles from Spectradyne sample buffers which could be considered.
Filter the solution with a Whatman Anotop 10, 0.02 µm syringe-driven filter into clean plastic tubes.
Select a cartridge type to load your sample into: *Note: all information in this cartridge selection table was based on experience in our lab up to May 2020, and needs to be continually re-evaluated

Calculate the volumes of filtered 1 % (v/v) Tween/PBS, spike-in beads, and sample particles to mix together. It is suggested that the final concentation of spike-in beads be 5E9 particles/mL*, the final concentration of sample particles be around 1E10 particles/mL* and that the overall volume is at least 50 µL to allow sufficient mixing. For C-300 cartridges use a 203 nm NIST PS spike-in bead For C-400 cartridges use a 269 nm NIST PS spike-in bead *These values were listed in May 2020 and may need to be updated at a future date based on future experience with a variety of samples
Thoroughly vortex spike-in beads (for example, 10 short pulses at max power) and pipette into 1 % (v/v) Tween/PBS buffer. Vortex the tube thoroughly to avoid any bead aggregates.
Vortex sample particle sample then pipette into the buffer/bead mixture. Vortex well.
Obtain a new sample cartridge and a P10 pipette set to 4 µL .
Vortex sample particle mixture immediatlely before drawing up 4 µL of sample and inspecting filled pipette tip to ensure the absence of air bubbles.
Using one hand to hold the cartridge in place, insert pipette tip into the fluid loading aperature until it gently contacts the bottom of the loading chamber.

While slowly pulling the pipette tip upward so that it is not contacting any surface load ~ 3.5 µL of the sample into the bottom of a sample cartridge, so that a small volume of sample is left in the tip and an air bubble isn't pipetted into the cartridge fluid well.

Tap the cartridge against the table surface to dislodge any bubbles then hold it at eye level to inspect the fluid level and check for an even meniscus absent of any bubbles. and then insert onto cartridge stage.
If there is a bubble, try alternative ways of tapping the cartridge, or, use the P10 pipette to aspirate the fluid out, several times, and retry.
Load the cartridge onto the stage and ensure both the blue indicator dot and the blue ring light are illuminated.

Use the nCS1 Cartridge Log document on SharePoint to record detailed information about each sample run on the instrument. This information is critical for identifying measurement quality over time. This step is specific for the NCI Translational Nanobiology Section's use.We do, however, recommend creating a log of your cartridge and its lot number to track performance over time.
Enter mold ID and box ID information into the nCS1 software.
Enter a descriptive prefix for the sample and a sample description if desired.
Create a new folder on the PC to store mesaurement data with date and descriptive name. Select the new folder as the file location for the measurement.
Visually confirm the stage is raised so that the cartridge is properly positioned to receive fluidic inputs.

Press "GO!" to begin automated procedure to prime cartridge. If the instrument has not had a cleaning cartridge run, the first priming procedure will take longer than the subsequent primings. (update as of 7/27/20: if the instrument is failing to prime, press "Stop" and then press "Clear constriction" and then try priming again).
After priming, the instrument will automatically begin taking 10 second acquisition measurements (also known as to as "traces".)

The blue line indicates the instrument voltage. Sharp spikes indicated particles pasing through the nanoconstriction and red Xs indicate events that the software determines to be particle clogs of the nanoconstriction. A clog-free trace will automatically be processed by the auto-analysis engine from a raw file into a stats file, which will then be plotted as a "Live CSD." If the number of transient clogs excedes a threshold, the software will not "flag" the raw file from the trace for processing and a stats files will not be automatically generated for that trace.

Using the auto-analysis engine's live CSD tool, particles within a diameter range can be selected and the concentration within the range displayed. This tool can be used to determine how long to acquire data, for example, until 10,000 spike-in beads have been measured. If the live CSD tool does not appear (may happen if the program needs to be restarted due to a crash), just click "Restart Auto-analysis Engine" under the Manual Utilities and be sure to continue processing accumulated files.

After the user is satisfied with the data collected, press the Stop button to end data collection. If the instrument clogs, as indicated by the recent acquisitions covered in red Xs and data not saved, click "Stop", "Clear Constriction", and then "Continuous" when allowed. This should fix most clogging situations and combine the data from the previous half of the run.
Press the End Run button and allow end run sequence to procede. After the sequence is finished the blue ring light should be iluminated.
Press the metal button, remove and discard the used cartridge.

Samples can continue to be run on new cartridges as described until done with nCS1 session. While the next sample is running, it possible to go to the Data Viewer software and combine the files from the previous sample acquisition to save time. To do so, select the correct folder, select all raw files from the past run, bring them down into the lower "currently loaded" box. Click "Process All" under the Process Raw Data tab. This will create stat files from the raw data. For the Translational Nanobiology Section pipeline ensure all filters are removed before clicking 'Combine Data for All Loaded' under the "Data Output" tab.
Load a cleaning cartridge onto the stage and raise the stage.
Select the "Instrument shut down" button and follow the on-screen instructions.

It is recommended to leave the DI water bottles attached to the machine while it is off in order to keep the lines from outside contaminants, and to keep the O-rings from cracking and drying out readily.
%s %s
creator: Jennifer Jones
